Name: Chikorita
Effects: Heal 10 damage from this Pokémon.
Flavor-Text: It uses the leaf on its head to determine the temperature and humidity. It loves to sunbathe.

Name: Bayleef
Effects: Flip a coin. If heads, your opponent’s active Pokémon is now Paralyzed.
Flavor-Text: The buds that ring its neck give off a spicy aroma that perks people up.

Name: Meganium
Ability-Effect: If this Pokémon’s remaining HP is 50 or less, its attacks do 70 more damage to your opponent’s Active Pokémon (before applying Weakness and Resistance).
Effects: Heal from this Pokémon the same amount of damage you did to your opponent’s Active Pokémon.
Flavor-Text: Meganium’s breath has the power to revive dead grass and plants. It can make them healthy again.

Name: Seedot
Flavor-Text: When it dangles from a tree branch, it looks just like an acorn. It enjoys scaring other Pokémon.

Name: Kricketot
Effects: Search your deck for up to 3 [G] Pokémon, reveal them, and put them into your hand. Shuffle your deck afterward.
Flavor-Text: When its antennae hit each other, it sounds like the music of a xylophone.

Name: Kricketune
Effects: During your next turn, any damage done to the Defending Pokémon by attacks is increased by 60 (after applying Weakness and Resistance).
Flavor-Text: It signals its emotions with its melodies. Scientists are studying these melodic patterns.

Name: Petilil
Effects: Heal 10 damage from each of your Pokémon.
Flavor-Text: Since they prefer moist, nutrient-rich soil, the areas where Petilil live are known to be good for growing plants.

Name: Lilligant
Effects: If your opponent attaches an Energy card from his or her hand to the Defending Pokémon during his or her next turn, that Pokémon will be Asleep.
Flip 2 coins. This attack does 30 damage times the number of heads.
Flavor-Text: Even veteran Trainers face a challenge in getting its beautiful flower to bloom. This Pokémon is popular with celebrities.

Name: Durant
Effects: Discard the top card of your opponent’s deck.
If this Pokémon has any damage counters on it, discard the top 4 cards of your opponent’s deck.
Flavor-Text: They attack in groups, covering themselves in steel armor to protect themselves from Heatmor.

Name: Growlithe
Flavor-Text: It has a brave and trustworthy nature. It fearlessly stands up to bigger and stronger foes.

Name: Arcanine
Effects: Discard an Energy attached to this Pokémon.
Flavor-Text: Its magnificent bark conveys a sense of majesty. Anyone hearing it can’t help but grovel before it.

Name: Numel
Flavor-Text: The magma in its body reaches 2,200 degrees Fahrenheit. Its hump gets smaller when it uses Fire-type moves.

Name: Camerupt
Effects: Each player discards the top card of his or her deck. This attack does 60 more damage for each Energy card discarded in this way.
Flavor-Text: If angered, the humps on its back erupt in a shower of molten lava. It lives in the craters of volcanoes.

Name: Emboar-EX
Effects: Flip a coin until you get tails. This attack does 20 more damage for each heads.
Discard 2 Energy attached to this Pokémon.
ex-Rule: When a Pokémon-EX has been Knocked Out, your opponent takes 2 Prize cards.

Name: Heatmor
Effects: If this Pokémon has a Pokémon Tool card attached to it, this attack does 20 more damage.
Flavor-Text: It draws in air through its tail, transforms it into fire, and uses it like a tongue. It melts Durant and eats them.

Name: Psyduck
Flavor-Text: It has mystical powers but doesn’t recall that it has used them. That is why it always looks puzzled.

Name: Golduck
Effects: Discard a Special Energy attached to your opponent’s Active Pokémon.
Flavor-Text: The forelegs are webbed, helping to make it an adept swimmer. It can be seen swimming elegantly in lakes, etc.

Name: Golduck BREAK
Ability-Effect: As often as you like during your turn (before your attack), you may move a basic Energy from 1 of your Pokémon to another of your Pokémon.
BREAK-Rule: Golduck BREAK retains the attacks, Abilities, Weakness, Resistance, and Retreat Cost of its previous Evolution.

Name: Slowpoke
Effects: Search your deck for a Pokémon, reveal it, and put it into your hand. Shuffle your deck afterward.
Flavor-Text: It lazes vacantly near water. If something bites its tail, it won’t even notice for a whole day.

Name: Slowbro
Effects: Flip a coin. If heads, this attack does 50 more damage.
If you use this attack when you have only 1 Prize card left, you win this game.
Flavor-Text: When a Slowpoke went hunting in the sea, its tail was bitten by a Shellder. That made it evolve into Slowbro.

Name: Slowking
Ability-Effect: Once during your turn (before your attack), you may flip a coin. If heads, move an Energy from your opponent’s Active Pokémon to 1 of his or her Benched Pokémon.
Effects: During your next turn, this Pokémon’s Psych Up attack does 40 more damage (before applying Weakness and Resistance).
Flavor-Text: When its head was bitten, toxins entered Slowpoke’s head and unlocked an extraordinary power.

Name: Shellder
Effects: Flip a coin. If heads, this attack does 10 more damage.
Flavor-Text: Clamping on to an opponent reveals its vulnerable parts, so it uses this move only as a last resort.

Name: Shellder
Effects: Flip a coin. If heads, your opponent’s Active Pokémon is now Paralyzed. If tails, this attack does nothing.
Flavor-Text: Clamping on to an opponent reveals its vulnerable parts, so it uses this move only as a last resort.

Name: Cloyster
Effects: If this Pokémon evolved from Shellder during this turn, your opponent’s Active Pokémon is now Paralyzed.
Flavor-Text: Its shell is extremely hard. It cannot be shattered, even with a bomb. The shell opens only when it is attacking.

Name: Staryu
Flavor-Text: It appears in large numbers by seashores. At night, its central core flashes with a red light.

Name: Gyarados-EX
Effects: Flip a coin until you get tails. For each heads, search your deck for a [W] Energy card and attach it to this Pokémon. Shuffle your deck afterward.
This attack does 10 damage to each of your Benched Pokémon. (Don’t apply Weakness and Resistance for Benched Pokémon.)
ex-Rule: When a Pokémon-EX has been Knocked Out, your opponent takes 2 Prize cards.

Name: M Gyarados-EX
Effects: You may do 20 more damage for each [W] Energy attached to this Pokémon. If you do, discard the top 2 cards of your deck.
Mega-Evolution-Rule: When 1 of your Pokémon becomes a Mega Evolution Pokémon, your turn ends.
ex-Rule: When a Pokémon-EX has been Knocked Out, your opponent takes 2 Prize cards.

Name: Lapras
Effects: Search your deck for a Trainer card, reveal it, and put it into your hand. Shuffle your deck afterward.
Flip a coin. If heads, this attack does 20 more damage.
Flavor-Text: Its high intelligence enables it to understand human speech. It likes to ferry people on its back.

Name: Corsola
Effects: Flip 2 coins. This attack does 30 damage times the number of heads.
Flavor-Text: They prefer unpolluted southern seas. Their coral branches lose their color and deteriorate in dirty water.

Name: Suicune
Ability-Effect: As long as this Pokémon is your Active Pokémon, prevent all effects of your opponent’s attacks, except damage, done to each of your Pokémon. (Existing effects are not removed.)
Flavor-Text: Said to be the embodiment of north winds, it can instantly purify filthy, murky water.

Name: Palkia-EX
Effects: Search your deck for 2 [W] Energy cards and attach them to 1 of your Benched Pokémon. Shuffle your deck afterward.
This attack does 30 damage to 1 of your opponent’s Benched Pokémon. (Don’t apply Weakness and Resistance for Benched Pokémon.)
ex-Rule: When a Pokémon-EX has been Knocked Out, your opponent takes 2 Prize cards.

Name: Manaphy-EX
Ability-Effect: Each of your Pokémon that has any [W] Energy attached to it has no Retreat Cost.
Effects: Heal 30 damage from each of your Benched Pokémon.
ex-Rule: When a Pokémon-EX has been Knocked Out, your opponent takes 2 Prize cards.

Name: Tympole
Effects: This attack does 10 damage times the number of your Pokémon in play that have the Round attack.
Flavor-Text: By vibrating its cheeks, it emits sound waves imperceptible to humans. It uses the rhythm of these sounds to talk.

Name: Palpitoad
Effects: Flip a coin. If heads, this attack does 20 more damage.
Flavor-Text: It lives in the water and on land. It uses its long, sticky tongue to immobilize its opponents.

Name: Seismitoad
Effects: Attach 3 Energy cards from your discard pile to this Pokémon.
Your opponent’s Active Pokémon is now Confused. That Pokémon can’t retreat during your opponent’s next turn.
Flavor-Text: They shoot paralyzing liquid from their head bumps. They use vibration to hurt their opponents.

Name: Ducklett
Effects: Flip a coin. If tails, this attack does nothing.
Flavor-Text: They are better at swimming than flying, and they happily eat their favorite food, peat moss, as they dive underwater.

Name: Swanna
Effects: This Pokémon does 20 damage to itself.
Flavor-Text: Swanna start to dance at dusk. The one dancing in the middle is the leader of the flock.

Name: Froakie
Effects: Flip a coin. If heads, your opponent’s Active Pokémon is now Paralyzed.
Flavor-Text: It protects its skin by covering its body in delicate bubbles. Beneath its happy-go-lucky air, it keeps a watchful eye on its surroundings.

Name: Frogadier
Effects: Search your deck for up to 3 Frogadier and put them onto your Bench. Shuffle your deck afterward.
Flavor-Text: Its swiftness is unparalleled. It can scale a tower of more than 2,000 feet in a minute’s time.

Name: Greninja
Effects: Until the end of your opponent’s next turn, each Pokémon your opponent has in play, in his or her hand, and in his or her discard pile has no Abilities. (This includes cards that come into play on that turn.)
You may return a [W] Energy from this Pokémon to your hand. If you do, this attack does 20 more damage.
Flavor-Text: It appears and vanishes with a ninja’s grace. It toys with its enemies using swift movements, while slicing them with throwing stars of sharpest water.

Name: Greninja BREAK
Ability-Effect: Once during your turn (before your attack), if this Pokémon is your Active Pokémon, you may discard a [W] Energy card from your hand. If you do, put 6 damage counters on 1 of your opponent’s Pokémon.
BREAK-Rule: Greninja BREAK retains the attacks, Abilities, Weakness, Resistance, and Retreat Cost of its previous Evolution.

Name: Electabuzz
Flavor-Text: It loves to feed on strong electricity. It occasionally appears around large power plants and so on.

Name: Electivire
Flavor-Text: It pushes the tips of its two tails against the foe, then lets loose with over 20,000 volts of power.

Name: Shinx
Effects: Your opponent switches his or her Active Pokémon with 1 of his or her Benched Pokémon.
Flavor-Text: All of its fur dazzles if danger is sensed. It flees while the foe is momentarily blinded.

Name: Luxio
Effects: Flip a coin. If heads, your opponent’s Active Pokémon is now Paralyzed.
Flavor-Text: Strong electricity courses through the tips of its sharp claws. A light scratch causes fainting in foes.

Name: Luxray
Effects: During your opponent’s next turn, any damage done by attacks from the Defending Pokémon is reduced by 20 (after applying Weakness and Resistance).
Flavor-Text: Luxray’s ability to see through objects comes in handy when it’s scouting for danger.

Name: Luxray BREAK
Effects: Flip a coin until you get tails. This attack does 40 more damage for each heads.
BREAK-Rule: Luxray BREAK retains the attacks, Abilities, Weakness, Resistance, and Retreat Cost of its previous Evolution.

Name: Blitzle
Effects: This Pokémon does 10 damage to itself.
Flavor-Text: Its mane shines when it discharges electricity. They use the frequency and rhythm of these flashes to communicate.

Name: Zebstrika
Ability-Effect: Damage from the attacks of your [L] Pokémon isn’t affected by any effects on your opponent’s Active Pokémon.
Effects: If your opponent’s Active Pokémon has [F] Resistance, this attack does 60 more damage.
Flavor-Text: They have lightning-like movements. When Zebstrika run at full speed, the sound of thunder reverberates.

Name: Drowzee
Effects: Flip a coin. If heads, this attack does 20 more damage.
Flavor-Text: If you think that you had a good dream but you can’t remember it, a Drowzee has probably eaten it.

Name: Hypno
Ability-Effect: Once during your turn (before your attack), you may leave both Active Pokémon Asleep.
Flavor-Text: Seeing its swinging pendulum can induce sleep in three seconds, even in someone who just woke up.

Name: Espeon-EX
Effects: Devolve each of your opponent’s evolved Pokémon and put the highest Stage Evolution card on it into your opponent’s hand.
This attacks’s damage isn’t affected by any effects on your opponent’s Active Pokémon.
ex-Rule: When a Pokémon-EX has been Knocked Out, your opponent takes 2 Prize cards.

Name: Skorupi
Effects: Flip 4 coins. This attack does 10 damage times the number of heads.
Flavor-Text: It burrows under the sand to lie in wait for prey. Its tail claws can inject its prey with a savage poison.

Name: Drapion
Effects: Your opponent’s Active Pokémon is now Poisoned.
Flavor-Text: It has the power in its clawed arms to make scrap of a car. The tips of its claws release poison.

Name: Sigilyph
Effects: During your opponent’s next turn, if this Pokémon is damaged by an opponent’s attack (even if this Pokémon is Knocked Out), put 5 damage counters on the Attacking Pokémon.
Your opponent reveals his or her hand.
Flavor-Text: The guardians of an ancient city, they always fly the same route while keeping watch for invaders.

Name: Trubbish
Effects: Flip a coin. If heads, discard an Energy attached to your opponent’s Active Pokémon.
Flavor-Text: Inhaling the gas they belch will make you sleep for a week. They prefer unsanitary places.

Name: Garbodor
Ability-Effect: If this Pokémon has a Pokémon Tool card attached to it, each Pokémon in play, in each player’s hand, and in each player’s discard pile has no Abilities (except for Garbotoxin).
Effects: Your opponent’s Active Pokémon is now Confused and Poisoned.
Flavor-Text: Consuming garbage makes new kinds of poison gases and liquids inside their bodies.

Name: Espurr
Flavor-Text: It has enough psychic energy to blast everything within 300 feet of itself, but it has no control over its power.

Name: Meowstic
Effects: Attach up to 2 Energy cards from your hand to 1 of your Benched Pokémon.
Flavor-Text: The eyeball patterns on the interior of its ears emit psychic energy. It keeps the patterns tightly covered because that power is too immense.

Name: Honedge
Effects: Put 1 damage counter on your opponent’s Active Pokémon.
Flavor-Text: If anyone dares to grab its hilt, it wraps a blue cloth around that person’s arm and drains that person’s life energy completely.

Name: Doublade
Effects: Put 3 damage counters on your opponent’s Active Pokémon.
Flavor-Text: The complex attack patterns of its two swords are unstoppable, even for an opponent greatly accomplished at swordplay.

Name: Aegislash
Effects: Double the number of damage counters on each of your opponent’s Pokémon.
This attack does 10 damage to 2 of your opponent’s Benched Pokémon. (Don’t apply Weakness and Resistance for Benched Pokémon.)
Flavor-Text: Apparently, it can detect innate qualities of leadership. According to legend, whoever it recognizes is destined to become king.

Name: Skrelp
Effects: Flip a coin. If heads, prevent all effects of attacks, including damage, done to this Pokémon during your opponent’s next turn.
Flavor-Text: It looks just like rotten kelp. It hides from foes while storing up power for its evolution.

Name: Phantump
Effects: Search your deck for a card that evolves from this Pokémon and put it onto this Pokémon. (This counts as evolving this Pokémon.) Shuffle your deck afterward.
Flavor-Text: According to old tales, these Pokémon are stumps possessed by the spirits of children who died while lost in the forest.

Name: Trevenant
Ability-Effect: As long as this Pokémon is your Active Pokémon, the attacks of your opponent’s Basic Pokémon cost [C] more.
Effects: This attack does 10 more damage for each Energy attached to your opponent’s Active Pokémon.
Flavor-Text: Using its roots as a nervous system, it controls the trees in the forest. It’s kind to the Pokémon that reside in its body.

Name: Trevenant BREAK
Effects: Put 3 damage counters on each of your opponent’s Pokémon.
BREAK-Rule: Trevenant BREAK retains the attacks, Abilities, Weakness, Resistance, and Retreat Cost of its previous Evolution.

Name: Sudowoodo
Effects: If your opponent’s Pokémon used an attack during his or her last turn, use it as this attack.
Flavor-Text: It disguises itself as a tree to avoid attack. It hates water, so it will disappear if it starts raining.

Name: Gible
Effects: Discard a card from your hand. If you do, draw 2 cards.
Flavor-Text: It nests in small, horizontal holes in cave walls. It pounces to catch prey that stray too close.

Name: Gabite
Effects: The Defending Pokémon can’t retreat during your opponent’s next turn.
Flavor-Text: As it digs to expand its nest, it habitually digs up gems that it then hoards in its nest.

Name: Garchomp
Effects: Attach an Energy card from your discard pile to 1 of your Pokémon.
If your opponent’s Active Pokémon is a Pokémon-EX, this attack does 80 more damage.
Flavor-Text: It flies at speeds equals to a jet fighter plane. It never allows its prey to escape.

Name: Pancham
Flavor-Text: It does its level best to glare and pull a scary face, but it can’t help grinning if anyone pats its head.

Name: Nuzleaf
Effects: Flip a coin. If tails, this attack does nothing.
Flavor-Text: It lives deep in forests. With the leaf on its head, it makes a flute whose song makes listeners uneasy.

Name: Shiftry
Effects: Flip 3 coins. If any of them are heads, your opponent reveals his or her hand. Then, for each heads, discard a card from your opponent’s hand.
Put a Trainer card from your discard pile into your hand.
Flavor-Text: By flapping its leafy fan, it can whip up gusts of 100 feet per second that can level houses.

Name: Darkrai-EX
Effects: This attack does 20 more damage for each [D] Energy attached to all of your Pokémon.
If your opponent’s Active Pokémon is Asleep, this attack does 80 more damage.
ex-Rule: When a Pokémon-EX has been Knocked Out, your opponent takes 2 Prize cards.

Name: Pangoro
Effects: Switch this Pokémon with 1 of your Benched Pokémon. During your opponent’s next turn, any damage done by attacks from the Defending Pokémon is reduced by 60 (before applying Weakness and Resistance).
This attack’s damage isn’t affected by Resistance.
Flavor-Text: It charges ahead and bashes its opponents like a berserker, uncaring about any hits it might take. Its arms are mighty enough to snap a telephone pole.

Name: Scizor-EX
Effects: During your opponent’s next turn, any damage done to this Pokémon by attacks is reduced by 20 (after applying Weakness and Resistance).
If this Pokémon was on your Bench and became your Active Pokémon this turn, this attack does 60 more damage.
ex-Rule: When a Pokémon-EX has been Knocked Out, your opponent takes 2 Prize cards.

Name: M Scizor-EX
Effects: You may discard a Special Energy attached to your opponent’s Active Pokémon or a Stadium card in play.
Mega-Evolution-Rule: When 1 of your Pokémon becomes a Mega Evolution Pokémon, your turn ends.
ex-Rule: When a Pokémon-EX has been Knocked Out, your opponent takes 2 Prize cards.

Name: Mawile
Effects: Flip a coin. If heads, your opponent’s Active Pokémon is now Paralyzed.
Flavor-Text: Attached to its head is a huge set of jaws formed by horns. It can chew through iron beams.

Name: Ferroseed
Effects: During your opponent’s next turn, if this Pokémon would be damaged by an attack, prevent that attack’s damage done to this Pokémon if that damage is 60 or less.
Flavor-Text: It absorbs the iron it finds in the rock while clinging to the ceiling. It shoots spikes when in danger.

Name: Ferrothorn
Effects: This attack does 10 damage to each of your opponent’s Pokémon for each [C] in that Pokémon’s Retreat Cost. (Don’t apply Weakness and Resistance for Benched Pokémon.)
Flavor-Text: They attach themselves to cave ceilings, firing steel spikes at targets passing beneath them.

Name: Clefairy
Effects: Your opponent’s Active Pokémon is now Asleep.
Flip 2 coins. This attack does 10 damage times the number of heads.
Flavor-Text: The moonlight that it stores in the wings on its back apparently gives it the ability to float in midair.

Name: Clefable
Effects: During your opponent’s next turn, prevent all effects of attacks, including damage, done to this Pokémon by [N] Pokémon.
Flip a coin. If heads, this attack does 30 more damage.
Flavor-Text: A timid fairy Pokémon that is rarely seen, it will run and hide the moment it senses people.

Name: Togekiss-EX
Effects: You may attach an Energy card from your hand to 1 of your Benched Pokémon.
Flip 4 coins. This attack does 50 damage times the number of heads.
ex-Rule: When a Pokémon-EX has been Knocked Out, your opponent takes 2 Prize cards.

Name: Spritzee
Flavor-Text: In the past, rather than using perfume, royal ladies carried a Spritzee that would waft a fragrance they liked.

Name: Aromatisse
Effects: If your opponent’s Active Pokémon is a Pokémon-EX, this attack does 40 more damage.
Flavor-Text: Its scent is so overpowering that, unless a Trainer happens to really enjoy the smell, he or she will have a hard time walking alongside it.

Name: Dragalge
Effects: Your opponent’s Active Pokémon is now Poisoned. Put 4 damage counters instead of 1 on that Pokémon between turns.
Discard the top card of your deck.
Flavor-Text: Tales are told of ships that wander into seas where Dragalge live, never to return.

Name: Rattata
Effects: Draw a card. Switch this Pokémon with 1 of your Benched Pokémon.
Flavor-Text: Its fangs are long and very sharp. They grow continuously, so it gnaws on hard things to whittle them down.

Name: Raticate
Ability-Effect: This Pokémon can’t be affected by any Special Conditions. (Remove any Special Conditions affecting this Pokémon.)
Effects: Your opponent’s Active Pokémon is now Poisoned. Discard all Pokémon Tool cards attached to that Pokémon.
Flavor-Text: The webs on its hind legs enable it to cross rivers. It searches wide areas for food.

Name: Raticate BREAK
Effects: Put damage counters on your opponent’s Active Pokémon until its remaining HP is 10.
BREAK-Rule: Raticate BREAK retains the attacks, Abilities, Weakness, Resistance, and Retreat Cost of its previous Evolution.

Name: Dunsparce
Effects: Search your deck for a Basic Pokémon and put it onto your Bench. Shuffle your deck afterward.
Flavor-Text: It digs into the ground with its tail and makes a mazelike nest. It can fly just a little.

Name: Stantler
Effects: Flip a coin. If heads, your opponent’s Active Pokémon is now Confused.
If you have any Mega Evolution Pokémon on your Bench, this attack does 50 more damage.
Flavor-Text: The curved antlers subtly change the flow of air to create a strange space where reality is distorted.

Name: Ho-Oh-EX
Ability-Effect: Once during your turn (before your attack), if this Pokémon has any basic [R] attached to it, you may heal 50 damage from it.
Effects: This attack does 30 damage to 1 of your opponent’s Benched Pokémon. (Don’t apply Weakness and Resistance for Benched Pokémon).
ex-Rule: When a Pokémon-EX has been Knocked Out, your opponent takes 2 Prize cards.

Name: Glameow
Effects: Your opponent puts a card from his or her hand on the bottom of his or her deck.
Flavor-Text: When it’s happy, Glameow demonstrates beautiful movements of its tail, like a dancing ribbon.

Name: Purugly
Effects: Flip a coin. If heads, this attack does 40 more damage. If tails, your opponent’s Active Pokémon is now Paralyzed.
Flavor-Text: To make itself appear intimidatingly beefy, it tightly cinches its waist with its twin tails.

Name: Furfrou
Effects: Draw a card.
Flip a coin. If heads, your opponent’s Active Pokémon is now Paralyzed.
Flavor-Text: Historically, in the Kalos region, these Pokémon were the designated guardians of the king.

Name: All-Night Party
Description: Once during each player’s turn, if that player’s Active Pokémon is Asleep, he or she may remove that Special Condition and heal 30 damage from that Pokémon.
Stadium-Rule: This card stays in play when you play it. Discard this card if another Stadium card comes into play. If another card with the same name is in play, you can’t play this card.

Name: Bursting Balloon
Move-Energy: If the Pokémon this card is attached to is your Active Pokémon and is damaged by an opponent’s attack (even if that Pokémon is Knocked Out), put 6 damage counters on the Attacking Pokémon.
Description: If this card is attached to 1 of your Pokémon, discard it at the end of your opponent’s turn.
Tool-Rule: Attach a Pokémon Tool to 1 of your Pokémon that doesn’t already have a Pokémon Tool attached to it.

Name: Delinquent
Description: Discard any Stadium card in play. If you do, your opponent discards 3 cards from his or her hand.
Supporter-Rule: You may play only 1 Supporter card during your turn (before your attack).

Name: Fighting Fury Belt
Description: The Basic Pokémon this card is attached to gets +40 HP and its attacks do 10 more damage to your opponent’s Active Pokémon (before applying Weakness and Resistance).
Tool-Rule: Attach a Pokémon Tool to 1 of your Pokémon that doesn’t already have a Pokémon Tool attached to it.

Name: Great Ball
Description: Look at the top 7 cards of your deck. You may reveal a Pokémon you find there and put it into your hand. Shuffle the other cards back into your deck.
Item-Rule: You may play as many Item cards as you like during your turn (before your attack).

Name: Gyarados Spirit Link
Description: Your turn does not end if the Pokémon this card is attached to becomes M Gyarados-EX.
Tool-Rule: Attach a Pokémon Tool to 1 of your Pokémon that doesn’t already have a Pokémon Tool attached to it.

Name: Max Elixir
Description: Look at the top 6 cards of your deck and attach a basic Energy card you find there to a Basic Pokémon on your Bench. Shuffle the other cards back into your deck.
Item-Rule: You may play as many Item cards as you like during your turn (before your attack).

Name: Max Potion
Description: Heal all damage from 1 of your Pokémon. If you do, discard all Energy attached to that Pokémon.
Item-Rule: You may play as many Item cards as you like during your turn (before your attack).

Name: Misty’s Determination
Description: Discard a card from your hand. If you do, look at the top 8 cards of your deck and put 1 of them into your hand. Shuffle the other cards back into your deck.
Supporter-Rule: You may play only 1 Supporter card during your turn (before your attack).

Name: Pokémon Catcher
Description: Flip a coin. If heads, switch 1 of your opponent’s Benched Pokémon with his or her Active Pokémon.
Item-Rule: You may play as many Item cards as you like during your turn (before your attack).

Name: Potion
Description: Heal 30 damage from 1 of your Pokémon.
Item-Rule: You may play as many Item cards as you like during your turn (before your attack).

Name: Professor Sycamore
Description: Discard your hand and draw 7 cards.
Supporter-Rule: You may play only 1 Supporter card during your turn (before your attack).

Name: Psychic’s Third Eye
Description: Your opponent reveals his or her hand. Discard as many cards as you like from your hand. Then, draw that many cards.
Supporter-Rule: You may play only 1 Supporter card during your turn (before your attack).

Name: Puzzle of Time
Description: You may play 2 Puzzle of Time cards at once.
Item-Rule: You may play as many Item cards as you like during your turn (before your attack).

Name: Reverse Valley
Description: Choose which way this card faces before you play it. Any damage done to this ↓ player’s [M] Pokémon by an opponent’s attack is reduced by 10 (after applying Weakness and Resistance).Choose which way this card faces before you play it. The attacks of this ↓ player’s [D] Pokémon do 10 more damage to your opponent’s Active Pokémon (before applying Weakness and Resistance).
Stadium-Rule: This card stays in play when you play it. Discard this card if another Stadium card comes into play. If another card with the same name is in play, you can’t play this card.

Name: Scizor Spirit Link
Description: Your turn does not end if the Pokémon this card is attached to becomes M Scizor-EX.
Tool-Rule: Attach a Pokémon Tool to 1 of your Pokémon that doesn’t already have a Pokémon Tool attached to it.

Name: Tierno
Description: Draw 3 cards.
Supporter-Rule: You may play only 1 Supporter card during your turn (before your attack).

Name: Splash Energy
Description: This card can only be attached to [W] Pokémon. This card provides [W] Energy only while this card is attached to a [W] Pokémon.If the [W] Pokémon this card is attached to is Knocked Out by damage from an opponent’s attack, put that Pokémon into your hand. (Discard all cards attached to it.)(If this card is attached to anything other than a [W] Pokémon, discard this card.)

Name: Gyarados-EX
Effects: Flip a coin until you get tails. For each heads, search your deck for a [W] Energy card and attach it to this Pokémon. Shuffle your deck afterward.
This attack does 10 damage to each of your Benched Pokémon. (Don’t apply Weakness and Resistance for Benched Pokémon.)
ex-Rule: When a Pokémon-EX has been Knocked Out, your opponent takes 2 Prize cards.

Name: M Gyarados-EX
Effects: You may do 20 more damage for each [W] Energy attached to this Pokémon. If you do, discard the top 2 cards of your deck.
Mega-Evolution-Rule: When 1 of your Pokémon becomes a Mega Evolution Pokémon, your turn ends.
ex-Rule: When a Pokémon-EX has been Knocked Out, your opponent takes 2 Prize cards.

Name: Manaphy-EX
Ability-Effect: Each of your Pokémon that has any [W] Energy attached to it has no Retreat Cost.
Effects: Heal 30 damage from each of your Benched Pokémon.
ex-Rule: When a Pokémon-EX has been Knocked Out, your opponent takes 2 Prize cards.

Name: Espeon-EX
Effects: Devolve each of your opponent’s evolved Pokémon and put the highest Stage Evolution card on it into your opponent’s hand.
This attacks’s damage isn’t affected by any effects on your opponent’s Active Pokémon.
ex-Rule: When a Pokémon-EX has been Knocked Out, your opponent takes 2 Prize cards.

Name: Darkrai-EX
Effects: This attack does 20 more damage for each [D] Energy attached to all of your Pokémon.
If your opponent’s Active Pokémon is Asleep, this attack does 80 more damage.
ex-Rule: When a Pokémon-EX has been Knocked Out, your opponent takes 2 Prize cards.

Name: Scizor-EX
Move-Damage: 20 Damage. During your opponent’s next turn, any damage done to this Pokémon by attacks is reduced by 20 (after applying Weakness and Resistance).
Effects: If this Pokémon was on your Bench and became your Active Pokémon this turn, this attack does 60 more damage.
ex-Rule: When a Pokémon-EX has been Knocked Out, your opponent takes 2 Prize cards.

Name: M Scizor-EX
Effects: You may discard a Special Energy attached to your opponent’s Active Pokémon or a Stadium card in play.
Mega-Evolution-Rule: When 1 of your Pokémon becomes a Mega Evolution Pokémon, your turn ends.
ex-Rule: When a Pokémon-EX has been Knocked Out, your opponent takes 2 Prize cards.

Name: Ho-Oh-EX
Ability-Effect: Once during your turn (before your attack), if this Pokémon has any basic [R] attached to it, you may heal 50 damage from it.
Effects: This attack does 30 damage to 1 of your opponent’s Benched Pokémon. (Don’t apply Weakness and Resistance for Benched Pokémon).
ex-Rule: When a Pokémon-EX has been Knocked Out, your opponent takes 2 Prize cards.

Name: Skyla
Description: Search your deck for a Trainer card, reveal it, and put it into your hand. Shuffle your deck afterward.
Supporter-Rule: You may play only 1 Supporter card during your turn (before your attack).

Name: Gyarados-EX
Effects: Flip a coin until you get tails. For each heads, search your deck for a [W] Energy card and attach it to this Pokémon. Shuffle your deck afterward.
This attack does 10 damage to each of your Benched Pokémon. (Don’t apply Weakness and Resistance for Benched Pokémon.)
ex-Rule: When a Pokémon-EX has been Knocked Out, your opponent takes 2 Prize cards.

Name: Professor Sycamore
Description: Discard your hand and draw 7 cards.
Supporter-Rule: You may play only 1 Supporter card during your turn (before your attack).

Name: Delinquent
Description: Discard any Stadium card in play. If you do, your opponent discards 3 cards from his or her hand.
Supporter-Rule: You may play only 1 Supporter card during your turn (before your attack).

Name: Delinquent
Description: Discard any Stadium card in play. If you do, your opponent discards 3 cards from his or her hand.
Supporter-Rule: You may play only 1 Supporter card during your turn (before your attack).
